# BitMEX.Net Full AI Reference

This file is optimized for AI coding assistants. It maps the actual BitMEX.Net API shape, options, endpoint groups, symbols, quantities, and common implementation patterns.

## Identity

- Repository: `JKorf/BitMEX.Net`
- Package: `JKorf.BitMEX.Net`
- Current project version: `3.9.3`
- Base library: `CryptoExchange.Net`
- REST client: `BitMEXRestClient`
- Socket client: `BitMEXSocketClient`
- Credentials: `BitMEXCredentials(string key, string secret)`

BitMEX.Net is not organized by separate spot/futures client roots. All REST endpoints live under `ExchangeApi`, split into `ExchangeData`, `Account`, and `Trading`.

## Correct Setup

```csharp
using BitMEX.Net;
using BitMEX.Net.Clients;
using BitMEX.Net.Enums;
using BitMEX.Net.ExtensionMethods;

var restClient = new BitMEXRestClient(options =>
{
    options.ApiCredentials = new BitMEXCredentials("API_KEY", "API_SECRET");
    options.Environment = BitMEXEnvironment.Live;
});

var socketClient = new BitMEXSocketClient(options =>
{
    options.ApiCredentials = new BitMEXCredentials("API_KEY", "API_SECRET");
});
```

## Options

REST options:

- `BitMEXRestOptions.Environment`
- `BitMEXRestOptions.ApiCredentials`
- `BitMEXRestOptions.AutoTimestamp`, default `false`
- `BitMEXRestOptions.BrokerId`
- `BitMEXRestOptions.ExchangeOptions`

Socket options:

- `BitMEXSocketOptions.Environment`
- `BitMEXSocketOptions.ApiCredentials`
- `BitMEXSocketOptions.SocketSubscriptionsCombineTarget`, default `10`
- `BitMEXSocketOptions.ExchangeOptions`

Global defaults:

```csharp
BitMEXRestClient.SetDefaultOptions(options =>
{
    options.ApiCredentials = new BitMEXCredentials("API_KEY", "API_SECRET");
});

BitMEXSocketClient.SetDefaultOptions(options =>
{
    options.ApiCredentials = new BitMEXCredentials("API_KEY", "API_SECRET");
});
```

Dependency injection is available through `services.AddBitMEX(...)`.

## REST API Map

### ExchangeData

Use `restClient.ExchangeApi.ExchangeData` for public market, instrument, book, funding, liquidation, announcement, and asset-network metadata:

- `GetServerTimeAsync()`
- `GetActiveSymbolsAsync()`
- `GetSymbolsAsync(symbol, symbolFilter, filter, columns, reverse, startTime, endTime, offset, limit)`
- `GetActiveIntervalsAsync()`
- `GetCompositeIndexesAsync(symbol, ...)`
- `GetIndicesAsync()`
- `GetSymbolVolumesAsync()`
- `GetTradesAsync(symbol, symbolFilter, filter, columns, reverse, startTime, endTime, offset, limit)`
- `GetKlinesAsync(symbol, BinPeriod.OneMinute, symbolFilter, partial, filter, columns, reverse, startTime, endTime, offset, limit)`
- `GetExchangeStatsAsync()`
- `GetExchangeStatHistoryAsync()`
- `GetExchangeStatHistoryUSDAsync()`
- `GetSettlementHistoryAsync(symbol, ...)`
- `GetBookTickerHistoryAsync(symbol, ...)`
- `GetAggregatedBookTickerHistoryAsync(symbol, BinPeriod.OneMinute, ...)`
- `GetOrderBookAsync(symbol, limit)`
- `GetInsuranceAsync(asset, ...)`
- `GetFundingHistoryAsync(symbol, ...)`
- `GetAnnouncementsAsync(columns)`
- `GetUrgentAnnouncementsAsync(columns)`
- `GetAssetsAsync()`
- `GetAssetNetworksAsync()`
- `GetLiquidationsAsync(symbol, ...)`

There is no `GetTickerAsync` endpoint wrapper. Use `GetSymbolsAsync`/`GetActiveSymbolsAsync` for instrument/ticker-like current fields, and `GetBookTickerHistoryAsync` for quote history.

### Account

Use `restClient.ExchangeApi.Account` for authenticated user, wallet, margin, withdrawal, address, and API-key endpoints:

- `GetUserEventsAsync(fromId, limit)`
- `GetAccountInfoAsync()`
- `GetFeesAsync()`
- `GetDepositAddressAsync(asset, network)`
- `GetMarginStatusAsync(asset)`
- `GetQuoteFillRatioAsync(accountId)`
- `GetQuoteValueRatioAsync(accountId)`
- `GetTradingVolumeAsync()`
- `GetBalancesAsync(asset)`
- `GetBalanceHistoryAsync(asset, targetAccountId, reverse, offset, limit)`
- `GetBalanceSummaryAsync(asset, startTime, endTime)`
- `TransferAsync(asset, fromAccountId, toAccountId, quantity)`
- `WithdrawAsync(asset, network, quantity, address, memo, addressId, targetUserId, fee, text, otpToken)`
- `CancelWithdrawalAsync(transactId)`
- `SetIsolatedMarginAsync(symbol, isolatedMarginEnabled)`
- `SetRiskLimitAsync(symbol, riskLimit, targetAccountId)`
- `TransferMarginAsync(symbol, quantity, targetAccountId)`
- `GetSavedAddressesAsync()`
- `AddSavedAddressAsync(currency, network, address, name, note, skipConfirm, skip2FA, memo, otpToken)`
- `GetAddressBookSettingsAsync()`
- `GetApiKeyInfoAsync()`

### Trading

Use `restClient.ExchangeApi.Trading` for order, execution, trade history, position, and leverage operations:

- `PlaceOrderAsync(symbol, orderSide, orderType, quantity, price, timeInForce, executionInstruction, contingencyType, displayQuantity, stopPrice, pegOffsetValue, pegPriceType, clientOrderLinkId, clientOrderId)`
- `GetOrdersAsync(symbol, symbolFilter, filter, columns, reverse, startTime, endTime, offset, limit)`
- `EditOrderAsync(orderId, origClientOrderId, newClientOrderId, quantity, quantityRemaining, price, stopPrice, pegOffsetValue)`
- `CancelOrderAsync(orderId, clientOrderId)`
- `CancelOrdersAsync(orderIds, clientOrderIds)`
- `CancelAllOrdersAsync(targetAccountIds, symbol, filter)`
- `CancelAllAfterAsync(timeout)`
- `GetExecutionHistoryByDayAsync(symbol, day)`
- `GetUserExecutionsAsync(symbol, symbolFilter, filter, columns, reverse, startTime, endTime, offset, limit)`
- `GetUserTradesAsync(targetAccountIds, symbol, symbolFilter, filter, columns, reverse, startTime, endTime, offset, limit)`
- `GetPositionsAsync(filter, columns, limit)`
- `SetCrossMarginLeverageAsync(symbol, leverage, targetAccountId)`
- `SetIsolatedMarginLeverageAsync(symbol, leverage, targetAccountId)`

Order enums:

- `OrderSide.Buy`, `OrderSide.Sell`
- `OrderType.Limit`, `Market`, `StopMarket`, `StopLimit`, `MarketIfTouched`, `LimitIfTouched`, `Pegged`
- `TimeInForce.Day`, `GoodTillCancel`, `ImmediateOrCancel`, `FillOrKill`, `AtTheClose`
- `ExecutionInstruction.PostOnly`, `AllOrNone`, `MarkPrice`, `IndexPrice`, `LastPrice`, `Close`, `ReduceOnly`, `Fixed`, `LastWithinMark`

## Websocket API Map

Use `socketClient.ExchangeApi`.

Public subscriptions:

- `SubscribeToTradeUpdatesAsync(symbols, onMessage)`
- `SubscribeToKlineUpdatesAsync(symbols, BinPeriod.OneMinute, onMessage)`
- `SubscribeToBookTickerUpdatesAsync(symbols, onMessage)`
- `SubscribeToAggregatedBookTickerUpdatesAsync(symbols, BinPeriod.OneMinute, onMessage)`
- `SubscribeToSettlementUpdatesAsync(onMessage)`
- `SubscribeToOrderBookUpdatesAsync(symbols, onMessage)`
- `SubscribeToIncrementalOrderBookUpdatesAsync(symbols, IncrementalBookLimit.Top25, onMessage)`
- `SubscribeToLiquidationUpdatesAsync(onMessage)`
- `SubscribeToInsuranceUpdatesAsync(onMessage)`
- `SubscribeToSymbolUpdatesAsync(SymbolCategory.Spot, onMessage)`
- `SubscribeToSymbolUpdatesAsync(symbol, onMessage)`
- `SubscribeToFundingUpdatesAsync(symbols, onMessage)`
- `SubscribeToAnnouncementUpdatesAsync(onMessage)`
- `SubscribeToNotificationUpdatesAsync(onMessage)`

Private subscriptions:

- `SubscribeToBalanceUpdatesAsync(onMessage)`
- `SubscribeToTransactionUpdatesAsync(onMessage)`
- `SubscribeToPositionUpdatesAsync(onMessage)`
- `SubscribeToMarginUpdatesAsync(onMessage)`
- `SubscribeToOrderUpdatesAsync(onMessage)`
- `SubscribeToUserTradeUpdatesAsync(onMessage)`

Socket callbacks receive `DataEvent<T>`. Many BitMEX channels return arrays because updates can contain multiple rows.

## Symbols

BitMEX symbols are exchange-native:

- `XBTUSD` for the classic inverse perpetual
- `ETH_USDT` for a spot-style symbol example shown in the README
- `ETHUSDT` for a linear contract-style example when returned by BitMEX metadata

Use:

```csharp
var symbols = await restClient.ExchangeApi.ExchangeData.GetActiveSymbolsAsync();
var xbt = symbols.Data.SingleOrDefault(x => x.Symbol == "XBTUSD");
```

Avoid:

```text
BTC-USDT
BTC/USDT
tBTCUSD
SpotApiV3
UsdFuturesApiV3
FuturesApiV2
```

## Quantities And Conversion

BitMEX uses base-unit currency quantities for wallets and some spot trading. Examples:

- `XBt` is the base unit representation for Bitcoin quantities.
- `gwei` can represent Ethereum base units.
- Derivatives generally use contract quantities.

Before using conversion helpers:

```csharp
var symbolInfo = await BitMEXUtils.UpdateSymbolInfoAsync();
if (!symbolInfo.Success)
    return;
```

Convert human asset/symbol amounts:

```csharp
var bitmexSpotQuantity = 0.01m.ToBitMEXSymbolQuantity("ETH_USDT");
var humanSpotQuantity = bitmexSpotQuantity.ToSharedSymbolQuantity("ETH_USDT");
var bitmexAssetQuantity = 0.01m.ToBitMEXAssetQuantity("ETH");
var humanAssetQuantity = bitmexAssetQuantity.ToSharedAssetQuantity("gwei");
```

Do not pass `0.01m` directly as a BitMEX spot `quantity` unless the API really expects `0.01` for that instrument. In this library, `PlaceOrderAsync` takes `long? quantity`.

## Result Handling

```csharp
var result = await restClient.ExchangeApi.ExchangeData.GetOrderBookAsync("XBTUSD", 25);
if (!result.Success)
{
    Console.WriteLine($"BitMEX error: {result.Error}");
    return;
}

Console.WriteLine(result.Data.Asks.Length);
```

For sockets:

```csharp
var sub = await socketClient.ExchangeApi.SubscribeToOrderBookUpdatesAsync("XBTUSD", update =>
{
    Console.WriteLine(update.Data.Symbol);
});

if (!sub.Success)
{
    Console.WriteLine(sub.Error);
    return;
}

await socketClient.UnsubscribeAsync(sub.Data);
```

## Shared Clients

BitMEX.Net implements CryptoExchange.Net shared APIs:

- `restClient.ExchangeApi.SharedClient`
- `socketClient.ExchangeApi.SharedClient`

Supported trading modes include spot, linear/inverse perpetuals, and linear/inverse delivery contracts. Shared clients may call `BitMEXUtils.UpdateSymbolInfoAsync()` internally to map symbols and quantities.

## Local State Helpers

Use built-in helpers instead of manually maintaining complex local state:

- `BitMEXOrderBookFactory`
- `BitMEXSymbolOrderBook`
- `BitMEXTrackerFactory`
- `BitMEXUserSpotDataTracker`
- `BitMEXUserFuturesDataTracker`

## Known Nonexistent Patterns

These are wrong for BitMEX.Net:

```csharp
client.SpotApi.ExchangeData.GetTickerAsync("BTC_USDT");
client.UsdFuturesApi.Trading.PlaceOrderAsync(...);
client.ExchangeApi.Trading.GetOpenOrdersAsync("XBTUSD");
new BitMEXCredentials("key", "secret", "passphrase");
KlineInterval.OneMinute;
OrderType.Stop;
```

Use:

```csharp
client.ExchangeApi.ExchangeData.GetSymbolsAsync("XBTUSD");
client.ExchangeApi.Trading.PlaceOrderAsync("XBTUSD", OrderSide.Buy, OrderType.Limit, quantity: 100, price: 50000m);
client.ExchangeApi.Trading.GetOrdersAsync("XBTUSD");
new BitMEXCredentials("key", "secret");
BinPeriod.OneMinute;
OrderType.StopMarket;
```
